learner_pain: Beginners feel anxious and self-conscious about speaking the new language with real people, so they avoid speaking altogether and never build the habit
how_common: ~65% of beginners say fear of sounding foolish stops them speaking
current_handling: No dedicated support; help center suggests finding a language partner

learner_pain: Learners hit a grammar point they don't understand mid-lesson and have no one to ask in the moment, so they stall and drift away
how_common: Very common; a top reason cited in day-1 drop-off surveys
current_handling: Static grammar-tips pages; no in-the-moment help

learner_pain: Learners want to know whether a sentence they wrote sounds natural to a native speaker, not just whether it is technically correct
current_handling: Exercises mark answers right or wrong against a fixed answer key

learner_pain: Advanced learners want to debate abstract topics like politics and ethics to keep their fluency sharp
how_common: Small but vocal segment (~3% of users)
current_handling: Advanced reading lessons only; no open practice

learner_pain: Learners aren't sure which words they should review and when, so they forget vocabulary they thought they had learned
current_handling: A basic review list that is not personalized to each learner's forgetting curve

learner_pain: Learners can't tell what their subscription includes or how to cancel it, leading to angry support tickets and chargebacks
current_handling: Help-center FAQ plus human support agents

learner_pain: The app is slow and crashes on older Android phones, so many beginners churn on day 1 before finishing a lesson
current_handling: Open item in the engineering backlog

learner_pain: Free-tier learners hit the daily lesson cap and get frustrated before the habit forms
Learners want a certificate accredited by a recognized institution to show employers
current_handling: Lingo issues an unaccredited completion badge

learner_pain: Streak and reminder notifications arrive at inconvenient times; learners want control over when they are nudged
current_handling: A fixed notification schedule with limited settings

learner_pain: Beginners can't tell whether they are pronouncing words correctly and get no feedback on their own attempts
current_handling: Audio clips to imitate; no feedback on the learner's own pronunciation

learner_pain: New learners don't know which level or course to start with, pick the wrong one, and quickly feel lost
current_handling: Learners self-select a level from a dropdown menu
